import numpy as np
from PIL import Image

img = Image.open\('override_final.png'\)
print\(f'override_final.png: {img.size}, mode={img.mode}'\)
arr = np.array\(img.convert\('L'\)\)
total = arr.size
zero  = \(arr == 0\).sum\(\)
print\(f'Zone 0: {zero:,} px  \({zero*100/total:.2f}%\)'\)
print\(f'Non-zero: {total-zero:,} px  \({\(total-zero\)*100/total:.2f}%\)'\)
unique, counts = np.unique\(arr, return_counts=True\)
print\(f'Unique values: {len\(unique\)}'\)
print\('Top 10 by count:'\)
for cnt, val in sorted\(zip\(counts, unique\), reverse=True\)[:10]:
    print\(f'  val {val:3d}: {cnt:,} px  \({cnt*100/total:.2f}%\)'\)
")

Bash("C:\\Users\\nicho\\AppData\\Local\\Python\\pythoncore-3.14-64\\python.exe" - << 'PYEOF'
import numpy as np
import rasterio
from rasterio.windows import Window

TIF = r"C:\\Users\\nicho\\minecraft-worldgen\\masks\\override_smoothed.tif"

with rasterio.open\(TIF\) as src:
    H, W = src.height, src.width
    print\(f"Size: {W}x{H}"\)

    # Test 1: check for banding — compare row-mean zone values
    # Sample a 512-row vertical strip at column centre
    print\("\\n--- Banding check: mean zone per 256-row block \(col 24576–25088\) ---"\)
    for r in range\(0, 2048, 256\):
        win = Window\(24576, r, 512, 256\)
        tile = src.read\(1, window=win\).astype\(np.float32\)
        print\(f"  rows {r:5d}–{r+256:5d}: mean={tile.mean\(\):.1f}  min={int\(tile.min\(\)\)}  max={int\(tile.max\(\)\)}  nonzero={int\(\(tile>0\).mean\(\)*100\)}%"\)

    # Test 2: orientation check — compare tile \(48,48\) with nearby tiles
    print\("\\n--- Tile \(48,48\) zone distribution ---"\)
    win = Window\(48*512, 48*512, 512, 512\)
    t4848 = src.read\(1, window=win\)
    vals, cnts = np.unique\(t4848, return_counts=True\)
    for v, c in sorted\(zip\(vals, cnts\), key=lambda x: -x[1]\):
        print\(f"  zone {v:3d}: {c*100//\(512*512\):3d}%"\)

    # Test 3: compare TIF x/z orientation to height.tif
    print\("\\n--- Orientation cross-check vs height.tif ---"\)
    with rasterio.open\(r"C:\\Users\\nicho\\minecraft-worldgen\\masks\\height.tif"\) as hsrc:
        hw = Window\(48*512, 48*512, 512, 512\)
        h4848 = hsrc.read\(1, window=hw\)
        ocean_mask = h4848 > 17050  # raw > sea level = ocean
        ocean_pct = int\(ocean_mask.mean\(\) * 100\)
        print\(f"  height tile\(48,48\): {ocean_pct}% ocean pixels"\)
        print\(f"  override tile\(48,48\): {int\(\(t4848==0\).mean\(\)*100\)}% zone-0 pixels"\)
        # If both are consistently ocean/land in the same places, orientation matches
        agreement = int\(np.logical_not\(np.logical_xor\(ocean_mask, t4848==0\)\).mean\(\)*100\)
        print\(f"  ocean/land agreement: {agreement}%  \(100%=perfect, ~50%=backwards\)"\)
PYEOF)

Bash("C:\\Users\\nicho\\AppData\\Local\\Python\\pythoncore-3.14-64\\python.exe" - << 'PYEOF'
import numpy as np
import rasterio
from rasterio.windows import Window

TIF = r"C:\\Users\\nicho\\minecraft-worldgen\\masks\\override_smoothed.tif"

with rasterio.open\(TIF\) as src:
    # Read a 3-tile-tall strip centred on tile \(48,48\)
    # Rows 47*512=24064 to 50*512=25600, cols 47*512=24064 to 50*512=25600
    win = Window\(24064, 24064, 1536, 1536\)
    strip = src.read\(1, window=win\).astype\(np.int32\)

print\(f"Strip shape: {strip.shape}"\)

# Check for horizontal banding: is there a 256-row repeating seam?
# Look at row-to-row variation
row_means = strip.mean\(axis=1\)
# Find rows where the change from previous row is unusually large
diffs = np.abs\(np.diff\(row_means\)\)
top_diff_rows = np.argsort\(diffs\)[-20:]
print\("\\nTop 20 rows with largest mean-zone jump \(potential seams\):"\)
for r in sorted\(top_diff_rows\):
    print\(f"  rel_row {r:4d} \(abs {r+24064:5d}\): Δmean={diffs[r]:.2f}  \({row_means[r]:.1f}→{row_means[r+1]:.1f}\)"\)

# Check if seams fall at multiples of 256
print\("\\nDo biggest jumps cluster at multiples of 256 \(chunk seams\)?"\)
seam_rows = [r for r in top_diff_rows if abs\(diffs[r]\) > diffs.mean\(\) + diffs.std\(\)]
at_256 = [r % 256 for r in seam_rows]
print\(f"  top jump rows mod 256: {sorted\(at_256\)}"\)

# Also check vertical lines \(column seams\)
col_means = strip.mean\(axis=0\)
col_diffs = np.abs\(np.diff\(col_means\)\)
top_col_rows = np.argsort\(col_diffs\)[-10:]
print\(f"\\nTop 10 column jumps \(for comparison\): {sorted\([int\(col_diffs[c]*10\)/10 for c in top_col_rows]\)}"\)
print\(f"Top 10 row jumps:                      {sorted\([int\(diffs[r]*10\)/10 for r in np.argsort\(diffs\)[-10:]]\)}"\)
PYEOF)

Bash("C:\\Users\\nicho\\AppData\\Local\\Python\\pythoncore-3.14-64\\python.exe" - << 'PYEOF'
import sys, io
sys.stdout = io.TextIOWrapper\(sys.stdout.buffer, encoding='utf-8', errors='replace'\)
import numpy as np
import rasterio
from rasterio.windows import Window

TIF = r"C:\\Users\\nicho\\minecraft-worldgen\\masks\\override_smoothed.tif"

with rasterio.open\(TIF\) as src:
    win = Window\(24064, 24064, 1536, 1536\)
    strip = src.read\(1, window=win\).astype\(np.int32\)

row_means = strip.mean\(axis=1\)
diffs = np.abs\(np.diff\(row_means\)\)
top20 = np.argsort\(diffs\)[-20:]
print\("Top 20 rows with largest mean-zone jump:"\)
for r in sorted\(top20\):
    print\(f"  rel_row {r:4d}  abs={r+24064:5d}  mod256={r%256:3d}  jump={diffs[r]:.2f}"\)

print\("\\nDo jumps cluster at mod256?"\)
print\(f"  mod256 values of top 20: {sorted\([r%256 for r in top20]\)}"\)

col_means = strip.mean\(axis=0\)
col_diffs = np.abs\(np.diff\(col_means\)\)
print\(f"\\nMax row jump: {diffs.max\(\):.2f}   Max col jump: {col_diffs.max\(\):.2f}"\)
print\(f"Mean row jump: {diffs.mean\(\):.3f}  Mean col jump: {col_diffs.mean\(\):.3f}"\)
PYEOF)
